item.title: Does Private Credit Work in ETFs? `Absolutely Not,′ Says DoubleLine
item.description: DoubleLine’s Sherman says private assets don’t belong in open-ended ETFs

item.title: Thiel’s Founders Fund Backs AI Cow Collar Startup at $2 Billion Valuation
item.description: Halter, a startup making artificial intelligence-powered collars for cows, is in talks to raise a new funding round that would double its valuation to more than $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Paraguay Holds Key Rate at 5.5%, Signals Neutral Stance
item.description: Paraguay’s central bank kept its benchmark interest rate steady at 5.5%, citing below-target inflation and an economy growing at a slower rate than last year.

item.title: Traders Turn Positive on US Dollar for First Time This Year
item.description: Traders turned positive on the US dollar for the first time this year as war in the Middle East jolts energy costs higher and supports the currency’s status as a haven.

item.title: Argentina Economy Grew Less Than Expected in Fourth Quarter
item.description: Argentina’s economy expanded less than analysts anticipated at the end of 2025, marking a slowdown even as President Javier Milei tries to stimulate growth.

item.title: Lula Says Petrobras, Pemex Could Partner on Oil Exploration
item.description: Brazil President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva said he spoke with Mexican leader Claudia Sheinbaum about a possible exploration partnership between their nations’ state-run oil companies in the Gulf of Mexico.

item.title: Markets Raise Bets on Bank of Canada Hikes as Oil Fears Mount
item.description: Markets see the Bank of Canada hiking interest rates more aggressively this year amid surging oil prices and hawkish messaging from peer central banks.

item.title: Central Banker Sees ‘Major Threat’ to Colombia From Iran War
item.description: The war in the Middle East is a “major threat” to a Colombian economy, which already faced serious price pressures, according to a senior policymaker.

item.title: Kalshi Temporarily Banned From Operating in Nevada by State Judge
item.description: Kalshi Inc. was temporarily barred by a judge from offering its prediction market contracts in Nevada, after state regulators said the company didn’t have a gaming license.

item.title: Anduril Bets on Simple Factory to Mass-Produce Uncrewed Fighters
item.description: Anduril Industries is ready to roll its first uncrewed Fury fighter off a production line in Ohio this summer, producing the computer-brained plane with a novel techniques: made-by-hand assembly without complicated machinery or robotics.

item.title: The Oil Prices You See Don’t Tell the Market’s Real Story
item.description: Three weeks into the Iran war, there’s an ever-growing gap between the price of oil futures and supplies that determine costs for consumers in the real world.

item.title: Tanker of Russian Fuel Veers Away From Cuba as US Tweaks Rules
item.description: A tanker carrying a cargo of diesel believed to be bound for Cuba updated its destination to Trinidad and Tobago shortly after the US clarified that the communist-run island remains ineligible to receive Russian fuel.

item.title: Muni Market Rout Deepens as Iran War Stokes Inflation Concerns
item.description: Pressure is building in the US municipal bond market.

item.title: Italian Bonds Emerge as Europe’s Weak Spot on Carry Trade Unwind
item.description: Italy’s bonds have been hammered since the start of the Iran war, emerging as the weakest link in the euro area as investors unwind one of the market’s favorite trades.

item.title: Janus Living Shares Jump About 18% After $840 Million IPO
item.description: Janus Living Inc., a seniors-focused real estate investment trust, jumped nearly 18% in its trading debut Friday after the company raised $840 million in an initial public offering.

item.title: Iran Unwilling to Talk About Opening Hormuz While Under Attack
item.description: Iranian officials have become reluctant to even discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz as they focus on surviving the US-Israeli onslaught, according to a person involved in direct, high-level contacts with Tehran.

item.title: One Veteran Director Has Some Thoughts on How to Build Video Games Faster
item.description: Veteran game director Josh Sawyer thinks developers need to make fewer big changes during production

item.title: Dallas Fed Sees Hit to Global GDP if Strait of Hormuz Stays Shut
item.description: A halt in Strait of Hormuz oil shipments that persists through June due to the Iran war would reduce global economic growth by an annualized 2.9 percentage points in the second quarter, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

item.title: Trump Approves NextEra Gas Expansion to Meet Rising Power Demand
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. plans to build up to 10 gigawatts of natural gas-fired generation in Texas and Pennsylvania to meet soaring demand driven in part by the data center boom.

item.title: Saudi Crude Oil Shipments From Yanbu Ease Following Record Surge
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crude loadings from the port of Yanbu on the Red Sea have shown a modest pullback over the past two days, following a record ramp-up as Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China Has Already Notched its First Major Win From the Iran War
item.description: BYD sales are already surging

item.title: Reeves to Lose £3 Billion of Fiscal Buffer Amid Gilts Selloff
item.description: The surge in UK gilt yields on the back of the Iran war could cost Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves more than a 10th of her fiscal buffer by increasing the cost of servicing government debt, according to Bloomberg economists.

item.title: Bond Market’s Big 2026 Fed Bet Flipped on Its Head by Oil Surge
item.description: Bond traders are scrambling for a new strategy after the oil-driven inflation shock triggered by the war in Iran scuppered the popular bet on further interest-rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Echelon Data Centres Owners Said to Weigh Options Including Sale
item.description: The owners of Echelon Data Centres are considering options including selling part or all of their stakes in the Dublin-based company, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Soy Poised for Worst Week Since 2024 as US-China Meeting Delayed
item.description: Soybean futures were headed for the biggest weekly decline since July 2024 as American exports once again stall after US President Donald Trump postponed his meeting with Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

item.title: Lincoln National Said to Seek Deal to Cut Life Insurance Risk
item.description: Lincoln National Corp. is seeking a reinsurance deal that would shift billions of dollars of life insurance reserves off its balance sheet, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran War Hits Chinese, Russian Fertilizer Exports to Nigeria
item.description: China and Russia are delaying fertilizer shipments to Nigeria as the Iran war shakes up the global supply chain for the crucial crop nutrients.

item.title: Mexico Finance Chief Downplays Fiscal Effects of Oil Turmoil
item.description: Mexico’s Finance Minister Edgar Amador said the fiscal effects from rising oil prices due to the war in Iran will be neutral.

item.title: UK Energy Price Cap Seen Jumping 20% as Iran War Roils Market
item.description: The UK’s cap on energy prices is expected to surge 20% this summer, heaping more pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to soften the blow before it lands.

item.title: Billionaire Batista in Talks to Rescue Brazil Steel Tycoon
item.description: Brazilian magnate Joesley Batista is making a play for the cement unit of Benjamin Steinbruch’s troubled conglomerate CSN, according to people familiar with the talks.
